q: Which of the following assumptions is NOT part of the kinetic theory of ideal gases?
opts: Gas molecules are point masses with negligible volume
Molecules move in random motion obeying Newton’s laws
Molecules exert strong intermolecular forces at all distances
Collisions between molecules are perfectly elastic
exp: Ideal gas assumes no intermolecular forces except during collisions. Strong forces at all distances violate the model.

q: The average kinetic energy of a gas molecule is directly proportional to:
exp: From kinetic theory, average KE = (3/2)kT, so it depends only on absolute temperature.

q: The SI unit of the Boltzmann constant is:
exp: k relates energy per molecule per temperature, so unit is joule per kelvin.

q: For an ideal gas, pressure is caused due to:
exp: Pressure arises from momentum transfer during collisions with container walls.

q: If temperature of a gas is doubled (in Kelvin), the rms speed becomes:
exp: v_rms ∝ √T, so doubling T increases speed by √2.

q: Which quantity remains constant for all gases at the same temperature?
exp: Average kinetic energy depends only on temperature, not gas type.

q: The equation of state for an ideal gas is:
exp: Standard ideal gas equation relates pressure, volume, moles, and temperature.

q: Which speed is highest in Maxwell distribution?
exp: v_rms > v_avg > v_mp for Maxwell distribution.

q: If pressure is kept constant, volume of gas is directly proportional to:
exp: Charles law: V ∝ T at constant pressure.

q: The number of degrees of freedom for a monoatomic gas is:
exp: Monoatomic gases have only translational motion in 3 directions.

q: The rms speed of gas molecules is given by:
exp: v_rms = √(3RT/M), derived from kinetic theory.

q: If volume of gas is halved at constant temperature, pressure becomes:
exp: Boyle’s law: P ∝ 1/V at constant temperature.

q: Which gas law relates pressure and temperature at constant volume?
exp: Gay-Lussac law: P ∝ T at constant volume.

q: The mean free path increases when:
exp: Lower pressure means fewer collisions, so mean free path increases.

q: In kinetic theory, pressure depends on:
opts: Both mass and velocity of molecules
exp: P depends on momentum transfer: both mass and velocity matter.

q: For diatomic gas at room temperature, degrees of freedom are:
exp: Diatomic gas has 3 translational + 2 rotational degrees.

q: Which of the following is NOT a state variable of gas?
exp: Microscopic kinetic energy varies molecule to molecule, not a macroscopic state variable.

q: If absolute temperature becomes 4 times, rms speed becomes:
exp: v_rms ∝ √T, so √4 = 2.

q: Avogadro’s number represents:
exp: One mole contains Avogadro number of particles.

q: Which process occurs at constant temperature?
exp: Isothermal process occurs at constant temperature.

q: The kinetic theory equation for pressure is:
exp: Derived from molecular collisions: P = (1/3)ρv_rms².

q: Which factor does NOT affect mean kinetic energy of gas molecules?
exp: KE depends only on temperature, not gas type.

q: Most probable speed is given by:
exp: From Maxwell distribution, v_mp = √(2RT/M).

q: If number of molecules increases at constant V and T, pressure:
exp: From PV = NkT, P ∝ N at constant V and T.

q: Which gas shows maximum deviation from ideal behavior at high pressure?
exp: CO₂ has stronger intermolecular forces, so more deviation.

q: The ratio of specific heats γ is equal to:
exp: Gamma is defined as Cp/Cv.

q: In an isochoric process:
exp: Isochoric means constant volume.

q: If temperature decreases, rms speed:
exp: v_rms ∝ √T, so decreases with temperature.

q: The average translational kinetic energy per mole is:
exp: Per mole KE = (3/2)RT.

q: Which assumption leads to ideal gas behavior?
exp: Ideal gas assumes point molecules with negligible volume.

q: At constant pressure, if temperature increases, volume:
exp: Charles law: V ∝ T at constant pressure.

q: Root mean square speed depends on:
exp: v_rms depends only on absolute temperature and molecular mass.

q: Which gas law is a combination of Boyle and Charles law?
exp: PV = nRT combines all gas laws.

q: If pressure doubles and volume halves, temperature:
exp: PV ∝ T, so (2P)(V/2)=PV, so T doubles.

q: The slope of PV vs T graph for ideal gas is:
exp: From PV = nRT, PV/T = nR constant slope.

q: Which particle motion is considered in kinetic theory?
exp: Molecules move randomly in straight lines between collisions.

q: The pressure of gas is independent of:
exp: Pressure depends on V, T, N but not shape of container.

q: Which gas law is valid only for ideal gases?
exp: All basic gas laws approximate ideal behavior under conditions.

q: If molecular mass increases, rms speed:
exp: v_rms ∝ 1/√M, so increases in mass reduce speed.

q: At same temperature, which gas has highest rms speed?
exp: Lower molar mass gives higher rms speed.

q: The gas constant R has unit:
exp: Universal gas constant unit is energy per mole per Kelvin.

q: In Maxwell distribution curve, area under curve represents:
exp: Area under speed distribution gives total number of molecules.

q: Which process has no heat exchange?
exp: Adiabatic process: Q = 0.

q: If gas is compressed rapidly, process is generally:
exp: Rapid compression does not allow heat exchange, so adiabatic.

q: Which is correct relation?
exp: Cp is always greater than Cv due to work done in expansion.

q: Pressure of gas depends on:
opts: Temperature, volume, and number of molecules
exp: From PV = NkT, pressure depends on all three.

q: The kinetic theory assumes collisions are:
exp: Collisions conserve kinetic energy.

q: At constant temperature, gas pressure is inversely proportional to:
exp: Boyle’s law: P ∝ 1/V.

q: If density increases, rms speed:
exp: Higher density implies more mass per volume, reducing speed at same T.
